evidence.sanitizedResult: Home renders NexusOps; WorkflowProSurface is an in-app workspace view with Open Graph, Open Panels, Export Contract, Import Contract, and Apply Preview controls.

summary: Mapped route and API terrain relevant to workflow, auth, artifacts, sync, provider, and observability.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Relevant routes include /api/v1/agents/[agentId]/stream, /api/image-gen, /api/v1/artifacts, /api/v1/workflows/groups, /api/v1/workflows/runtime-trace, /api/v1/workspaces/session, /api/v1/workspaces/[workspaceId]/state, and sync/observability endpoints.

summary: Mapped Workflow Runtime Lite execution states and provider call boundaries.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Runner emits queued/running/success/failed node updates; LLM nodes call /api/v1/agents/:id/stream; image nodes call /api/image-gen and then attempt /api/v1/artifacts persistence.

summary: Mapped auth, permission, and idempotency gates on key API routes.

evidence.sanitizedResult: apiHandler requires auth when permission/auth is configured, mutation idempotency is on by default, artifact routes require workspace read/update permission, workspace session requires auth, and image-gen enforces auth/permission only in production runtime.

summary: Checked provider and Supabase credential presence without printing raw secrets.

evidence.sanitizedResult: .env.local exists with OPENAI_API_KEY present (sha256_12 recorded in command output only), OPENAI_IMAGE_MODEL=gpt-image-2, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL present, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY present; .env and ~/.gateway.env absent; shell env had no checked vars.

summary: Observed localhost first visible state after checkpoint using Browser.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Page title NEXUS // AI OPS; visible text sample shows IDENTITY GATE / GLOBAL VAULT with EMAIL, PASSWORD, LOGIN, Authenticate to unlock, and NEED ACCOUNT. Workflow Pro text was not visible in the first state.

summary: Inferred that Workflow Pro UI cannot be claimed usable until auth gate is crossed and Computer Use evidence records the path.

inference.claim: Workflow Pro exists in source but the post-checkpoint localhost first state is an auth gate, so UI usability remains not-yet-verified.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: Computer Use login/sign-up followed by visible Workflow Pro mode operation.

summary: Inferred that real provider checks are mandatory for image/LLM claims in this run.

inference.claim: Because configured OpenAI credentials and an image model are present, provider-backed workflow/image verdicts require real provider/API evidence or a blocked reason.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: Credential probe showing no usable provider credentials or route returning sanitized provider-blocking errors.

summary: Recorded source-versus-live-access contradiction for Workflow Pro visibility.

contradiction.nextProbe: Use Computer Use to authenticate or create an operator account, then attempt to switch to Workflow Pro and record visible result.

summary: Added risk that local image-gen can use server provider key without proving production account authorization parity.
Next probe is P02 auth/account/workspace authority with Computer Use screen operation on localhost.
nextProbe: Read checkpoint, start P02-core, use Computer Use on http://127.0.0.1:3000 to cross auth gate or record blocked state; then test route authority with sanitized local API probes.

summary: Completed P01-core terrain scan with static, script, and browser-live supporting evidence; Computer Use and real provider evidence still pending.
Read active checkpoint before P02-core authority exploration.
Started P02 core authority inventory for identity, workspace, route, artifact, and storage gates.
Observed localhost auth gate with Computer Use in the user's Chrome window.

sourceRefs: Computer Use: Google Chrome visible window at http://localhost:3000/
evidence.sanitizedResult: Opened a new Chrome tab, typed localhost:3000, pressed Return, and observed NEXUS auth gate with email field, password field, Login, and Need Account controls. No login submission was performed because Chrome displayed autofilled values that were not part of this run.

summary: Recorded unauthenticated local API authority matrix for workspace, artifact, sync, workflow, and provider-adjacent routes.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Unauthenticated GET /health and /public-config returned 200. Unauthenticated workspace session, workspace state, artifacts GET/POST, and sync status returned 401 AUTH_REQUIRED with sanitized request/trace ids recorded in command output. Empty image-gen prompt returned 400 before provider work.

summary: Recorded workflow group/runtime-trace method and auth behavior from localhost probes.

evidence.sanitizedResult: GET /api/v1/workflows/groups and /api/v1/workflows/runtime-trace returned 405. Minimal unauthenticated POST to both routes returned 401 AUTH_REQUIRED with request ids req_a0b53afb-d0b8-42de-b509-3dbaab7d3dc6 and req_130a53d3-47cc-4d39-92dd-e3d5be84ee4f.

summary: Created a throwaway Supabase account and exercised workspace session/state/artifact routes with its live session.

sourceRefs: Supabase auth signUp via project public config
evidence.sanitizedResult: Throwaway account signUp returned hasUser=true and hasSession=true; email sha256_12=0bb4f32e0528, user id sha256_12=b5fbe3cba22d. Authenticated POST /workspaces/session returned 200 role=owner, workspaceId=workspace_df5a977812ea4d508b, created=true, requestId=req_c3c20f41-7be0-4c4e-a3f0-4012f0426df5, traceId=trace_263cc04a-63b4-4e4b-a07e-943276adb1a2. Authenticated workspace state GET for that workspace returned 403 PERMISSION_DENIED requestId=req_27eaf5b2-7370-4324-bdb0-a0b93db88c70 traceId=trace_d96a5e38-e911-4768-a9ff-9249f851212b. Authenticated artifacts GET for that workspace returned 403 PERMISSION_DENIED requestId=req_c78a81f7-7ccd-4b95-b9be-af2d4653201d traceId=trace_6f81cd39-fd65-4882-9549-86a339d637a3.

summary: Mapped local workspace-session and permission service repository split from source.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Workspace session route requires auth, then in non-production without service role uses a local in-memory workspace session repository. Workspace state/artifact routes create a permission service from the request; with a bearer token and no service role they use the request-scoped Supabase client for membership reads, so local in-memory memberships from /workspaces/session are not shared with those routes.

summary: Separated UI account-flow status from API account-flow evidence.

inference.claim: Account creation is live at the Supabase/API level, but the visible localhost UI account flow remains not-yet-verified because Computer Use did not submit the auth form.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: Computer Use creates or logs into an account through the UI and visibly reaches the NEXUS workspace without exposing secrets.

summary: Inferred likely cause for session-owner versus downstream-permission mismatch.

inference.claim: The most likely local failure mode is that /workspaces/session creates a local in-memory workspace membership while state/artifact permission checks read Supabase memberships through the authenticated request client, so the same bearer token sees owner on session creation but 403 on downstream reads.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: A migration/RPC or request-scoped repository path that proves the workspace membership is persisted to the same store used by downstream permission checks, or a successful authenticated state/artifact read for the created workspace.

summary: Recorded owner workspace session versus downstream permission denial contradiction.

contradiction.evidenceB: E-P02-003 same session workspace state/artifacts 403 PERMISSION_DENIED
contradiction.nextProbe: Inspect workspace-session RPC/migrations and choose repair path so local authenticated account creation writes membership to the same authority store used by state/artifact permission checks.

summary: Added P0 risk that new accounts can be created yet cannot access their workspace state/artifacts on localhost.
Next probe is source/migration verification of workspace authority alignment, then P05 real provider test.
nextProbe: Read workspace membership migrations/RPC and permission service tests; then run a real provider-backed image route probe with sanitized output if auth/permission blockers do not prevent it.

summary: Workflow Pro UI remains not-yet-verified because Computer Use has not crossed the auth gate into the workspace.

verdict.claim: Visible Workflow Pro UI availability on localhost

summary: Completed P02-core authority pass with Computer Use auth-gate evidence, unauth route matrix, live account/API evidence, and a product-risk contradiction around workspace authority.
Read active checkpoint before P02-suspicion workspace authority root-cause verification.
Started source, migration, and test verification for workspace authority mismatch C-P02-001.
Verified workspace session RPC and route/service selection boundaries from source and migrations.

evidence.sanitizedResult: The RPC writes both workspaces and workspace_memberships and is granted to authenticated users. Existing code sends preview/production-without-service-role to authenticated RPC, but local runtime selects the local in-memory workspace session service. Existing tests cover preview RPC fallback and local repository fallback separately, not the live local runtime plus real Supabase bearer-token combination.

summary: Ran focused workspace session and permission tests.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Vitest result: 2 test files passed, 11 tests passed. This supports that existing intended preview/local behaviors are covered, but live localhost bearer-token authority split is not currently covered by these tests.

summary: Called workspace session RPC directly with a live Supabase session and then probed localhost downstream permissions.

sourceRefs: Supabase RPC nexus_ensure_workspace_session via user session
evidence.sanitizedResult: Throwaway account signUp had hasUser=true and hasSession=true; email sha256_12=75dcbd0ac89d, user id sha256_12=b4874b8b90d6. Direct RPC returned ok=true, role=owner, created=true, reason=created_user_workspace, workspaceId=workspace_b4c67653181d4999ae54e41a95682d55. Localhost state GET for that workspace returned 404 WORKSPACE_STATE_NOT_FOUND rather than 403. Localhost artifacts GET returned 200 with artifact list keys.

summary: Raised confidence that C-P02-001 is caused by local session route authority selection, not by the RPC migration itself.

inference.claim: Direct authenticated RPC creates workspace membership in the same store used by downstream permission checks; therefore the local session route's local in-memory fallback is the likely cause of the 200-owner then 403 downstream split.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: A local route implementation that also calls the RPC in this runtime yet still produces downstream 403, or a Supabase/RLS audit showing the direct RPC path did not actually create comparable membership.

summary: Recorded local session route versus direct RPC downstream-permission contrast.

contradiction.evidenceA: E-P02-003 local session route returned owner workspace then state/artifacts returned 403
contradiction.evidenceB: E-P02-007 direct RPC returned owner workspace then state returned 404 and artifacts returned 200
contradiction.nextProbe: Repair or test the session route so local runtime with a real bearer token prefers authenticated RPC, then rerun the live local route matrix.

summary: Workspace session local-route authority is a confirmed product-risk blocker for Workflow Pro persistence on localhost.

verdict.claim: Local authenticated workspace session route returns an authority result that downstream state/artifact routes cannot honor

summary: Completed P02-suspicion with source, migration, unit-test, and live RPC contrast evidence for C-P02-001/C-P02-002.
Read active checkpoint before P05-core provider, media, storage, and load inventory.
Started P05 core multimodal provider, storage, and load probe; real provider evidence is mandatory because OpenAI credentials are configured.
Mapped multimodal provider, media packet, storage, and artifact routes from source.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Image generation route uses server OPENAI_API_KEY or runtime authorization in development, calls OpenAI-compatible /images/generations through DalleImageAdapter when a key is present, materializes base64/image output into a local asset route, and uploads to Supabase storage only when accessToken and workspaceId are present. Artifact routes can save generated-image records and download durable stored bytes. Workflow Pro file/vision/audio paths currently carry references through ContextPacket/file-node boundaries; native vision/audio compiler behavior is represented as a future/no-op boundary in source fixtures.

summary: Ran real OpenAI image provider probe without workspace persistence.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Real image provider call returned status 200 in 15375 ms, mode=dall-e, media url kind=local_asset_route, generatedAsset provider=memory, durable=false, mime=image/png, sizeBytes=757152. Asset route fetch returned 200 image/png with 757152 bytes. Response content did not contain a raw data URL.

summary: Ran real OpenAI image provider probe with live Supabase session and durable storage upload.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Throwaway account signUp and RPC workspace succeeded; email sha256_12=5b743bd96be9, user sha256_12=885910aac4d2, workspaceId=workspace_6da770fd795046c0997a1a14a683793e. Real image call returned status 200 in 15618 ms, mode=dall-e, generatedAsset durable=true, provider=supabase-storage, mime=image/png, sizeBytes=816689, bucket/path present. Asset route fetch returned 200 image/png with 816689 bytes. No raw data URL was present in response content.

summary: Ran real provider to durable storage, saved generated-image artifact record, listed it, and downloaded artifact asset bytes.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Throwaway account and RPC workspace succeeded; email sha256_12=44cbd1243ec8, user sha256_12=bf93e36a2845, workspaceId=workspace_38b14a785bc74886a29e00389d8510c2. Real image call returned status 200 in 18438 ms, mode=dall-e, generatedAsset durable=true, provider=supabase-storage, mime=image/png, sizeBytes=729590. Artifact create returned 200 saved generated-image, requestId=req_7785c67d-427e-4734-953c-2209f3ce8022, traceId=trace_0404ad78-ff05-436b-bc2c-ed4df9847286, artifactId prefix=3d1264cf-868, contentSizeBytes=729590. Artifact list returned 200 count=1. Artifact asset download returned 200 image/png, byte length header=729590, byte length=729590, content disposition present.

summary: Ran live LLM provider verification through localhost provider verify route with mock fallback disabled.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Using `.env.local` OpenAI key in memory only, provider verify returned status 200 in 1428 ms, verified=true, provider=openai-compatible, model=gpt-4o-mini, checkedAt present. The provider key was not printed or persisted; key sha256_12=0fed9db8eec2.

summary: Separated provider success from durability and Workflow Pro UI usability claims.

inference.claim: Real image provider, storage upload, artifact record creation, artifact listing, and artifact asset download work through localhost API when the workspace membership is created through the authenticated RPC path; however this does not prove the visible Workflow Pro UI flow works because the UI remains behind the auth/workspace gate until C-P02-001 is repaired or bypassed in UI testing.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: Computer Use screen evidence showing a logged-in operator runs Workflow Pro image flow end to end, with generated history and download visible.

summary: Provider-backed LLM and image/storage/artifact behavior is locally live at API level, but Workflow Pro UI remains not-yet-verified.

verdict.claim: Localhost API provider-backed LLM verification and image generation with durable generated-image artifact storage

summary: Inferred the next highest-value multimodal work is UI-auth repair followed by a controlled Workflow Pro image-reverse-fanout run.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Focused Vitest run passed: 9 test files, 44 tests. Coverage includes route sanitization/permission behavior, Runtime Lite run state, trace client, durable group records, trace correlation, runtime evidence warnings, run group inspector, and group record client payloads.

summary: Ran live localhost runtime ledger API probe with real Supabase session and RPC workspace.

evidence.sanitizedResult: Throwaway account had live session; email sha256_12=a42a760fc70f, user sha256_12=5dfed156e95a, workspaceId=workspace_404b0c41764745ab87c3b5a1dd5e92a8. Artifact marker create returned 200 saved with requestId=req_fb049d16-f168-448f-9472-8ce195a5d254 and traceId=trace_5c7b23b8-254e-4e58-a8e4-9ca390f21ca5. Workflow group record returned 200 eventType=workflow.group_record.upserted, eventId prefix=63e2760b-6b03-, workflowGroupId=wf_group_p03_8263e8281708, workflowId=workflow_p03_2dee3f1edcb0, traceId=trace_p03_21e0dfbddd27, nodeCount=2, edgeCount=1. Runtime trace returned 200 eventType=workflow.runtime_lite.run.succeeded, eventId prefix=7efe3182-6cd1-, workflowRunId=run_p03_58a39e346cd5, same workflowGroupId and traceId, status=success.

summary: Separated API-level durable runtime trace success from UI-level liveness transparency.

inference.claim: Runtime durability is supported at scan/test/API-route level: outputs can be represented by durable artifacts, group records and runtime traces can be written with shared group/trace IDs, and the repo guards against completing output-producing tasks before durable persistence. Visible UI liveness, active-node progress, and reload recovery remain not-yet-verified because the UI auth/workspace gate is still blocked.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: Computer Use UI run evidence showing node state transitions, generated output ID, durable read path, and reload recovery, or a live route read showing written events cannot be recovered.

summary: Runtime group/trace durable ledger works at localhost API level but UI runtime liveness remains not-yet-verified.

verdict.claim: Localhost API can persist sanitized Workflow Pro group records and Runtime Lite run traces tied to durable output artifacts

The scan produced many matches and was summarized rather than copied to avoid noise.

summary: Created deployed-environment hardening note for future push/preview/production live probes.

evidence.sanitizedResult: The future hardening note identifies preview/production checks that would be stronger after push/deploy: Vercel protection boundary, app auth boundary, new account workspace authority, Supabase storage/RLS parity, production image-gen auth boundary, role matrix, generated history, and recovery diagnostics. This run did not claim deployed parity.

summary: Separated localhost evidence from deployed parity claims.

inference.claim: Localhost API success and local UI auth-gate evidence are not sufficient to prove preview or production behavior, especially where code branches on NODE_ENV/VERCEL_ENV, Vercel protection, service-role availability, production image-gen authorization, or Supabase RLS/storage policy.
inference.canBeFalsifiedBy: A current preview/production live probe that exercises the same route/account/action matrix with sanitized request IDs, trace IDs, and Computer Use screen evidence where UI claims are made.

summary: Preview and production parity are not yet verified in this run.
